nodes.summary: validate reads source_artifact_prune_globs_on_init from contract and scans working tree
validate collects violations and emits prune_glob_check section in upgrade_validate.json
validate sets summary.status = failure when violation_count > 0
validate emits stderr diagnostic per violation when violations found
prune glob scan skipped (status = skipped) when repo_mode != generated-consumer
prune glob scan skipped when contract cannot be loaded; existing contract_load_error gate covers this
postcheck reads prune_glob_check from validate report and surfaces prune_glob_violations section
postcheck adds prune-glob-violations to blocked_reasons when violation_count > 0
SKILL.md adds required check step after manual merge resolution naming canonical glob patterns
unit test: prune glob scan returns matching paths when file matches a glob
unit test: prune glob scan returns empty violations when no files match
unit test: prune glob scan returns status skipped when repo_mode = template-source
integration test: validate exits non-zero and report includes violations when matching files present
integration test: postcheck exits non-zero and includes prune-glob-violations in blocked_reasons
pathlib.Path.rglob() only; no shell expansion; no subprocess; no symlink traversal outside repo root
one stderr line per violation: prune-glob violation: <path> (matches: <glob>)
prune glob check set to skipped on contract load error; no additional failure contribution
remediation_hint names exact files to remove and command to re-run validation
validate fails with violations listed when generated-consumer contains files matching prune globs
validate succeeds with empty violations when no files match prune globs
postcheck blocks with prune-glob-violations in blocked_reasons when validate report contains violations
prune_glob_check.status = skipped for template-source repos; no failure contribution
SKILL.md contains required check step naming both glob patterns by value
